question_text: A microwave oven rated at 1000 W is 65% efficient at heating food. A conventional oven rated at 2000 W is 45% efficient. If you need to supply 10,000 J of energy to food, which appliance requires less electrical energy input and by how much?
correct_answer: The microwave requires less energy by approximately 6,154 J (or the microwave needs ~15,385 J compared to the oven's ~22,222 J)
explanation: For the microwave: useful energy = 65% of input, so 10,000 = 0.65 × input, giving input = 15,385 J. For the oven: 10,000 = 0.45 × input, giving input = 22,222 J. The microwave needs less total electrical energy because it's more efficient, even though it has lower power. Efficiency matters more than power rating when comparing energy requirements.
